tickets.body: Get help with subscription or billing

Dear Support Team,  I am writing to request assistance with my account and a specific document issue.  First, I would like to speak with a representative regarding my subscription. Although I signed up today, it appears my subscription was cancelled, which I did not authorize.  Additionally, I have a question about a document I received. I believe I was sent a template to sign while the document was still being created. When I attempted to upload this document into Libra, I received an error stating that the signatures could not be validated because the document is a digitally signed read-only copy or a read-only document.  Could you please help me resolve these issues?  Best regards,    Marnie Jones

tickets.body: Hello signNow Support / API Team,           We are implementing embedded signing via API (embedded invites + embedded signing links).             Our requirements are:              Create embedded signing links for signers with OTP authentication (e.g., SMS).     At a later time, we want the ability to trigger signNow to send an email invitation to only selected signers, without changing the already issued embedded link(s) (i.e., keep the same signing URL that the signer has).    Ideally, this “send email later” action should be possible per signer (not to all recipients at once), and should not require re-creating the invite.             Question:    Is it technically possible to start with embedded signing (link delivery) and later send signNow email invitations for specific signers without regenerating / changing the signing link?          Thank you.            Kind regards,     Michos Ioannis
tickets.subject: Embedded signing links with OTP + later email sending for selected signers without changing links

tickets.body: Hi SignNow Support,          I'm running into an issue when updating a document via the  PUT /document/{id}  endpoint while trying to configure conditional fields with a dependency using the equals operator.          I'm receiving the following validation error:    {    "errors": [    {    "code": 65536,    "message": "Failed to validate conditional fields, equals operator must have field1 and value only as attributes"    }    ]    }          This is visible in request ID 4e4b3d10052bf0d76b83ee4fcdf055c8.          I've tried structuring as    "dependency": {    "operator": "equals",    "parent": [    {    "field_name": "Radio Group 1",    "value": "Radio Button 1"    }    ]    }          Could you clarify the correct JSON schema for a dependency? Specifically:             What is the expected structure for each operator?      Should the referenced field use field_name, field1, or another attribute?      Are there any constraints on how radio group values should be referenced (label vs internal value)?             If helpful, I can provide a full GET /document/{id} response for the relevant fields.          Thanks,    Kieran             Kieran Maclagan      Software Developer              PCL Constructors Inc.     9915 56 Ave NW    Edmonton, AB T6E 5L7    T: 780-733-5037     kmaclagan@pcl.com               www.pcl.com       TOGETHER WE BUILD SUCCESS

I cancelled it immediately after signing my document, then deleted my account - there is absolutely no universe where over 40 dollars a month to sign a PDF is useful to anyone. I received an email confirming I would not be charged, but I knew that wasn't going to be the case, because I'm familiar with your business model that makes money off of subscriptions people don't notice they're being charged for. Hi,  We have a user within our SignNow  umich.org  that noticed a change in their SignNow experience this past Tuesday 4/28 which is causing them considerable confusion.. The issue they reported is       When I upload and send documents from the shared team folder, the completed documents are automatically saving under “Owned by Me” instead of remaining in the shared folder for the team.      This is a change from our normal workflow, as documents previously stayed in the shared folder so all team members could track sent and signed items.              They tell us they reached out directly to SignNow and were told-   this is controlled by a workspace-level setting:   “Directory where to save document copies sent from Team space.”   SignNow advised updating this setting to  Team Space/public (instead of personal)  so that sent documents remain in the shared team folder.        We "University of Michigan" have not completely switched over to workspaces for our org's, so I don't believe this 'fix' applies to our users. From my understanding from my team and their conversations with our representative Arti Harchekar, we do not have the admin aspects of workspaces yet.       Is it possible something was changed on the backside of this team    O & P Missing Doc and DM Footwear    or the team owner   chambest@umich.edu  , that you can change back?  Thank you.    David Sulick University of Michigan Business Systems Analyst Donor & Alumni Relationship Tool (DART), Document Imaging, E-Signature, Mcard & digital Asset Collaborative (DAC) Information and Technology Services
tickets.subject: SignNow Team Shared Team folder Issue

tickets.subject: Error

tickets.body:
Talk to human, here's transcript. Techncal error 
 Conversation with SignNow Started on May 22, 2026 at 03:02 PM Eastern Time (US & Canada) time EDT (GMT-0400) 
 --- 
 03:02 PM |  tech@tfc.com : Running into some document issues. Happened on May 14 for Malt Drive Pool Rider - Janice Li   Also happened for Malt Drive Pool Rider - Glendaliza Almonte today.   What's going on? 
 03:02 PM | Luna: Feel free to ask your question below. 
 03:02 PM | Luna: I checked our status page, and there are no active incidents right now, so this is unlikely to be a platform-wide outage.   For the “document issues” you saw on May 14 and again today, the most common cause is the PDF itself (for example: form fields, comments, layers, transparencies, or unsupported PDF types like password-protected/encrypted PDFs, PDF/A, or dynamic XFA forms). Flattening the PDF before upload (often “Print to PDF”) usually fixes shifting or rendering problems. ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13167315-uploading-documents-and-templates )   If you are seeing an error while opening or signing, try Incognito/Private mode and clear cache/cookies, and note your browser and version. ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13413898-best-devices-and-browsers-for-a-smooth-signnow-signing-experience )   I can help you narrow it down if you tell me what exactly happens (error message, or does the document look wrong, or can’t be opened).    Sources: Uploading Documents and Templates ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13167315-uploading-documents-and-templates ) Best Devices and Browsers for a Smooth SignNow Signing Experience ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13413898-best-devices-and-browsers-for-a-smooth-signnow-signing-experience ) 
 03:02 PM | Luna: Is that what you were looking for? 
 03:02 PM |  tech@tfc.com : These are the errors I got:   Exception: SignNow\Rest\EntityManager\Exception\EntityManagerException - Request failed! Reason: Client error: POST  https://api.signnow.com/document/fieldextract   [65656] Could not add fields to this document id - f5d7d4d73c23498a81515936d225558bea6d529a File: /releases/db604442-1779391722/vendor/signnow/rest-entity-manager/src/EntityManager.php, Line: 318 
 03:03 PM | Luna: That error means your API call to POST  https://api.signnow.com/document/fieldextract  failed to add fields to the document, so the document upload with field extraction did not complete successfully. ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/entities )   A few common causes to check:   - The document already has a pending invite or has been signed, which can block editing (fields can only be added to documents not sent to signers). ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/fields ) ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/guides-embedded-editor )  - If you are uploading via Postman Web, it can fail with “Must upload one file” unless you switch to the Desktop Agent (Auto-select agent off, select Desktop Agent, install it, then retry).   If you already have the document ID and a cURL request that reproduces it, I can help you narrow down which of the above is happening.    Sources: About documents, document groups, and templates | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/entities ) About fields | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/fields ) About the embedded editor | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/guides-embedded-editor ) 
 03:03 PM | Luna: Did that answer your question? 
 03:04 PM |  tech@tfc.com : It does it via the Signnow API or SDK or something. Look at Malt Drive Pool Rider - Glendaliza Almonte I can't even open it 
 03:04 PM | Luna: That error usually means the document is in a state where SignNow can’t add fields to it anymore, so the “field extract” upload fails. This happens when the document already has a pending invite or has been signed, because fields can only be added to documents that have not been sent to signers. ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/guides-embedded-editor ) ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/fields )   If you can’t even open the document in the web app, it’s often a PDF compatibility issue (password-protected/encrypted, PDF/A, dynamic XFA forms, portfolios, embedded attachments, or antivirus blocking). Flattening the PDF (often “Print to PDF”) before uploading usually fixes it. ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13167315-uploading-documents-and-templates )   If you already have the document ID, I can help you narrow down which of the above is happening. ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/entities )    Sources: Uploading Documents and Templates ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13167315-uploading-documents-and-templates ) Best Devices and Browsers for a Smooth SignNow Signing Experience ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13413898-best-devices-and-browsers-for-a-smooth-signnow-signing-experience ) About the embedded editor | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/guides-embedded-editor ) About fields | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/fields ) About documents, document groups, and templates | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/entities ) 
 03:04 PM | Luna: Was this helpful? 
 03:05 PM |  tech@tfc.com : Document ID is f5d7d4d73c23498a81515936d225558bea6d529a 
 03:05 PM | Luna: That document ID is failing because SignNow can’t add fields to it anymore, so the field-extract upload won’t complete. This usually happens when the document already has a pending invite or has been signed, since fields can only be added to documents that have not been sent to signers. ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/fields ) ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/guides-embedded-editor ) ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13163213-editing-documents-after-signatures-are-completed )   If you can’t even open it in the web app, it can also be a PDF compatibility issue (password-protected/encrypted, PDF/A, dynamic XFA forms, portfolios, embedded attachments, or antivirus blocking). Flattening the PDF (often “Print to PDF”) before uploading usually fixes it. ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13167315-uploading-documents-and-templates )   I can help you narrow down which of the above is happening if you already have the steps to reproduce and the exact error you see when opening it. tickets.body:
so what we did was not working in my prior ticket, but I wanted to make sure you understood what is happenning and who I need to reach out to fix this issue. Thank you again for your help; I want to explain the issue in more detail to make sure we’re aligned. Our workflow is the following: We go into our Team Shared Folder: “O & P Missing Doc and DM Footwear” We select a subfolder (for example: “Sent O&D/Waiver”) We click “Create” --> “Upload Documents” (this shows: “Upload or Create and add to ‘O & P Missing Doc and DM Footwear’ team”) We upload the document and send it to the patient Before Tuesday: The document would remain in the same shared folder we were working in This allowed our team to track all sent and completed documents in one place Now (starting Tuesday): After sending the document, it is automatically routed to our personal “Owned by Me” documents (Outbox) We now have to manually move each document back into the shared folder Important detail: This behavior is not consistent across users The Team Owner can follow the exact same steps, and their documents still remain in the shared folder. However, for myself and another team member (Avery) (who are "Admins" in the Team Group, the documents are routing to our personal folders instead. Additional note: Avery and I are able to move each other’s documents within the shared folders The Team Owner is not able to move our documents (which has always been the case) Because of this, it seems like documents are being created with different ownership depending on the user, even though we are all working within the same team and folder. This behavior changed suddenly on Tuesday without any changes on our end 
  ​
tickets.account: The Reqents of the University of Michigan

This behavior changed suddenly on Tuesday 4/28/26 without any changes on our end

tickets.body:
Hello, 
 I’m currently experiencing an issue when sending documents to patients from our “Teams” shared folder in SignNow. 
 When I upload and send a document from the team folder, it is automatically being saved under my personal “Owned by Me” documents instead of remaining in the shared team folder. 
 This is a change from our normal workflow, as documents previously stayed within the shared folder so all team members could track and manage them. 
 I’ve confirmed that I am in the correct team and folder when uploading, but the issue is still occurring. 
 Could you please help identify why this is happening and how to ensure documents remain in the shared team folder after being sent? 
 Thank you!
tickets.account: The Reqents of the University of Michigan

We have paid over $3,000 for our annual subscription, yet when I attempt to access the API portal ( spiros.c@lexingtonhl.net ), I am prompted to either start a free trial or subscribe — as if we are not a paying customer.  This is a compliance production account actively in use. For reference, Document Group ID dfb4d557111e4e2eb9a2053518bd75f8e431beae was created, sent, and signed as recently as 05/19/2026. I have the full audit log confirming continued usage under this account.  I need the following resolved immediately:  1.My admin login :  Spiros.C@LexingtonHL.net  and our registered company main account is - ( SignNOW.001@LexingtonHL.net ) properly linked to our paid company subscription so API access reflects our active plan. 2.Confirmation of our current subscription tier, renewal date, and the API entitlements included. 3.Direct contact information for our assigned Account Executive or Account Manager.  I have attempted to reach support by phone at 1-800-831-2050 (the number appears to be disconnected), through your online support portal, and via prior emails — none of which have resulted in a response.  Please treat this as urgent. We are a mortgage lending company (NMLS# 347811) and depend on this platform for legally binding e-signature workflows with borrowers. Continued inability to access API features we have paid for is directly impacting our operations.  Spiros Cheng Founder, Lexington Home Loans NMLS# 347811 Tel. 949.280.9893

After evaluating our operational needs, I decided to move to a more complete platform that combines document storage, client portal, tax preparation workflow, and electronic signature features in a single system. Dear Sir/Madam, We are OCCASIO TANK STORAGE BV, a reliable tank storage and logistics company based in the Netherlands, with operations in Rotterdam, Houston, Fujairah, and Jurong Port. If you require tank storage and logistics services, please do not hesitate to contact us. Our facilities in Rotterdam, Houston, Fujairah, and Jurong Port are equipped to receive crude oil and oil products via pipelines, railway, and vessels. We offer state-of-the-art capabilities for handling a wide range of liquid bulk products and customs services, including the loading and unloading of oil products. We are pleased to confirm the availability of Tank Storage Service for various petroleum products, including JET A1, EN590, D2, D6 Virgin Oil, and others, all at competitive rates directly from our ports. If you would like a quote for any of these products’ storage or receiving the Tank Storage Agreement (TSA) or Charter Party Agreement (CPA), kindly contact us with the details below. You can also contact us via email at  storage@occasiotankstorage.nl . Alternatively, you can reach us by phone or WhatsApp at + 31 9700-503-3844. Yours sincerely, Hendrik A Logan Director of Logistics.  OCCASIO TANK STORAGE BV Phone or WhatsApp at + 31 9700-503-3844.

PayPal Transaction details:     - 2024 (May 17, 2024): Transaction ID 1D555416D4852783Y – 96.00 USD  - 2025 (May 17, 2025): Transaction ID 637832770M3144235 – 96.00 USD  - 2026 (May 17, 2026): Transaction ID 3YY62766W3631471K – 96.00 USD     Total claimed: 288.00 USD     Important facts:     - I have never actively used your services, neither in 2024, nor in 2025, nor in 2026.  - I never received any prior renewal notification (as required by French consumer law, Article L.215-1, and the EU Directive 2011/83/EU) for any of these three annual renewals. I only received invoices after each payment.  - Therefore, none of these three automatic renewals were properly authorized. My consent was not informed and is considered invalid under European and French law.     As a result, I am entitled to a full refund of the last three renewal payments (2024, 2025 and 2026), according to the principle of répétition de l'indu (Article 1302-1 of the French Civil Code) and the 5-year statute of limitations (Article 2224).     What I need from you:     1. Full refund of 288.00 USD (96 USD × 3) to my PayPal account.  2. Immediate cancellation of any future automatic renewals associated with my email address:  moussasyllapro@gmail.com .     Please confirm the refund within 48 hours. If I do not receive a positive response within that time, I will immediately open a dispute with PayPal and file a chargeback with my bank (Caisse d'Épargne). Under EU payment regulations, my bank will refund me immediately for any unauthorized direct debit.     Thank you for your prompt handling of this matter.     Sincerely,     Moussa Sylla  PayPal account:  moussasyllapro@gmail.com   2026 transaction ID: 3YY62766W3631471K  Total amount claimed: 288.00 USD

We were reached out to by a Jack Spinola ( jspinola@cahill-sf.com ) about an invitation to a workspace that was sent to one of their employees by one of our employees. They state that “ rsaravia@cahill-sf.com ” received an invite from our “ jmcadory@balfourbeattyus.com ”. Jessica, CC’d here, did not send this invite, and after reviewing her SignNow account history, we see no evidence of compromise or sending of this invite.            Attached is the original email that  rsaravia@cahill-sf.com  received, which looks like a completely legitimate SignNow invitation. We do have a workspace called “balfourbeattyus.com” as well. tickets.body:
Hi SignNow Support,  We're a SignNow customer (Rock Rabbit) reaching out about an email deliverability issue we're seeing on outbound signature invites sent from  mail@signnow.com .  Summary Over the past week, three of our customers have independently reported that signature invite emails originating from  mail@signnow.com  are landing in their spam folders, and in at least one case the message was flagged by the recipient's email provider as "malicious." These are legitimate invites tied to documents our team sent through SignNow — not bulk or marketing email.  Because  mail@signnow.com  is a sender domain you own and control, there's nothing we can change on our DNS side (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) to address this. We're hoping you can help us understand what's going on and what options are available.  Specific questions 1. Is  mail@signnow.com  sent from a shared sending pool across SignNow customers? If so, have you seen recent reputation issues with the IPs or domain that pool uses — particularly with Microsoft / Outlook.com / Office 365, since the "marked as malicious" framing usually points to Microsoft's SmartScreen filter?  2. Do you offer a way to send signature invites from a customer-controlled subdomain (e.g.,  sign.rockrabbit.ai ) where we'd publish our own SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records and inherit our own sender reputation? Some eSignature providers offer this as "branded sending" or a "custom send-as domain."  3. If a shared pool is the only option, can you confirm the current SPF, DKIM, and DMARC posture for  mail@signnow.com , and whether there's a customer-side configuration we can use to improve deliverability (e.g., a different sender display name, reply-to setting, or branded subdomain on a higher tier)?  4. Are you currently aware of, or actively investigating, deliverability complaints from other customers in the past 1–2 weeks?  Account / impact - Account: Rock Rabbit (signing under our SignNow workspace) - Sender address recipients saw:  mail@signnow.com  - Reported by: 3 separate customers in the past week - Reported behavior: emails routed to spam; at least one inbox surfaced a "malicious" warning rather than a plain spam label - We can share message-IDs, recipient domains, and screenshots if helpful — let us know the best secure channel.  For context, I've already reviewed your KB article on preventing emails from going to spam ( https://www.signnow.com/help/prevent-emails-spam ). That guide is focused on content-side levers (subject lines, trigger words, whitelisting requests), which we'll continue to follow, but it doesn't address sender authentication or reputation — which is what we believe is actually driving these flags. 
   
 Thanks,  
 Glenn  
   
 --          
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
       
     rockrabbit.ai   
 
 
  Glenn Billman  
  Manager, Building Decarbonization & Operations  
 
 
 
   
 
  (650) 283 0124   
   glenn.billman@rockrabbit.ai

hi Luna  I need help with our Netsuite integration. Here is what happens (or more what not happens) 
 We send Estimate ES-38160 to the client for signature via Netsuite. The client did receive the request and signed the file. The signed PDF is available on SignNow (see screenshot: ES-38160_Status SignNow_SignNow.png) but it's not saved in Netsuite (see:ES-38160_Status SignNowNS_missing file.png). 
 The SignNow settings for Netsuite are 'save the signed document in Netsuite and in SIgnNow' (see screenshot: SignNow_DocumentStorage_NS) ​

Problem Description:    We are unable to send invites that include a custom subject and custom message when using the POST API. We are receiving the following error:          "message": "Upgrade your subscription plan to personalize invite subject and message."          Please find the attached screenshot for reference.    However, the same functionality works correctly when sending invites through the SignNow UI, where both the custom subject and message are successfully delivered to recipients.  {   "subject": "Need your signature",   "from": " borthakur.insurance@gmail.com ",   "to": [     {       "phone_invite": "+13056083756",       "role": "Signer 1",       "method": "sms",       "reminder": 5,       "role_id": "ROLE_1",       "expiration_days": 27,       "sms_message": "Hi, you have a document to sign.",       "authentication_type": "phone",       "order": 1     }   ],   "message": "Please review and sign the document" } 
 However, I am receiving the following response: 
 {   "errors": [     {       "code": 65582,       "message": "Invalid phone number"     }   ] }

Subject: Request for full plan terms disclosure — Account ID 15628117 
 Hello SignNow Support, 
 I am writing regarding my Business Annually subscription (Account ID: 15628117,  primelinea@gmail.com , invoice 0198b384-8a81-70cf-8897-99da70bb3fc0 dated 08/16/2025). 
 I have used 94 signature invites so far. My account dashboard indicates that a charge of €96 will be applied after 106 invites, which appears to be an overage / auto-renewal trigger I was not clearly informed about at the time of purchase. 
 I have three concerns: 
 1) The 100-invite-per-user limit and the €0.96 per-invite overage    rate are NOT disclosed on your public pricing page    (signnow.com/features/signnow-pricing). They are only referenced    indirectly in Terms of Service §8.2 as "units (for example    documents or transactions)", which a reasonable customer would    not understand as referring to signature invites. 
 2) The "Business Annually" plan is described in marketing as    "$8/user/month billed annually", with no mention of an invite    quota. This is a material term that affects the purchase decision    and, in my view, should be prominently disclosed. 
 3) The upcoming €96 charge will trigger before my annual renewal    date (08/16/2026), without an explicit consent step from me at    that moment. 
 I would like to request the following: 
 a) A written confirmation of the full terms of my specific plan:    - Exact invite quota per period    - Exact overage rate    - When and how auto-renewal / overage charges are triggered    - Cancellation policy and refund eligibility 
 b) Given the lack of clear upfront disclosure, I would appreciate    either (i) an increase of my invite quota for the current period    at no extra cost, or (ii) a waiver of the upcoming €96 overage    charge as a one-time goodwill gesture. 
 c) If neither (a) nor (b) is acceptable, please confirm in writing    that I may cancel auto-renewal immediately while retaining access    to the service until the paid-through date of 08/16/2026, with    no further charges. 
  ​

Please add 2 additional licenses to account, when you are able to do so.      To access your owner's portal, please visit  www.ghertner.com/owner .   For the fastest service, please submit all requests (including pool key, maintenance and architectural) and make payments through the owner portal. You may also email your requests directly to    hsr@ghertner.com   .

     Hi  Kathleen , 
 Thank you for reaching out to Google Play Support.  My name is Nova and I appreciate you for reaching out to us. I am writing to provide you with an update regarding your refund request. 
 I have carefully reviewed your request, and I sincerely wish I had better news to share. Unfortunately, the refund has been  declined  as it does not meet the criteria of Google Play’s current policies. I truly understand how disappointing this is, especially when you are looking for a swift resolution. 
  Next Steps with the Developer  To ensure you still have a path toward a resolution, I recommend contacting the app developer directly at   support@signnow.com  . As the creators of the game, they have the authority to review individual cases for their players and can often provide solutions that fall outside of our standard refund windows. 
 
 
 Response Time:  They typically respond within  3 business days . 
 
 Follow-up:  If you do not hear back from them after that time, please reach back out to us with a screenshot of your sent email, and we will be happy to look into the matter further. 
 
  Securing Your Account  To help prevent any accidental or unauthorized purchases, I highly recommend setting up  password or PIN protection  on your device. This adds a layer of security to your account and requires a password to complete any transaction. 
 Thank you for your understanding. If you have any further questions, please feel free to reply to this email. 
 Within 48 hours of our last interaction, you may receive a short survey via email. We’d love to hear your feedback about our interaction today and your overall experience with Google Support.

Dear SignNow Billing Department,  I am writing regarding an unauthorized automatic renewal charged to my account.  Your company renewed my subscription without my consent or authorization, despite the fact that we have not used SignNow in nearly two years, as the business associated with the account is no longer operating. In addition, I had previously expressed the desire to terminate and discontinue the subscription.  At no point did I actively approve or authorize this recent renewal charge.  Given the circumstances, I am requesting the following immediately:    Cancellation of the subscription effective immediately   Confirmation that no further charges will occur   A full refund of the unauthorized renewal charge    Please treat this matter as urgent and confirm in writing once the cancellation and refund have been processed.  If this issue is not resolved promptly, I will have no choice but to escalate the matter through my financial institution as an unauthorized recurring charge as well as file a complaint with the better business bureau. I canceled my subscription the same day 5/8/2026 "today"; basically 10 minutes after downloading my documents 2:53am. I went to Google play store as instructed by your app. And when I unsubscribed I get a message that's states my subscription will be cancelled on exactly May 15, 2026. Today is not May 15, 2026 and I would like to cancel my subscription now. If it gets canceled on May 15, 2026 then I will be charged the $19.99 and I do NOT want to be charged. Your app mentioned free 7 day trial and I just needed to app for a day. Please do not charge my card for $19.99 as I have canceled my subscription way before May 15, 2026.  Is there any other step I need to take to make sure I do not get charged?? Thank you for taking time to read my message. Please contact me is you have any possible questions. Hello,    I’m investigating an issue where documents opened through the SignNow embedded editor are showing in View Only mode with no field/editor menu available. This is happening in both our production and staging environments.    Below is a clean staging reproduction.    I uploaded a brand-new PDF in our application, then used our SignNow integration to create an embedded editor link. SignNow successfully created the document and returned an editor link, but opening that link showed the document in View Only mode.    I also opened the same document directly from the SignNow dashboard while signed in as the document owner, and it still showed View Only with no editor/field menu.    Staging document details:      SignNow document id:  49113734b2ca4147b82490375397f4b2ddee94a8 
 
  Document owner shown in SignNow metadata:   tfritz@torticity.com  
 
  Internal tracking id in our system:  1773 
 
  Source document id in our system:  26924 
 
  Lead/case id in our system:  4805 
 
    The SignNow document metadata appears to rule out the documented View Only causes:       field_invites: [] 
 
   signatures: [] 
 
   roles: [] 
 
   requests: [] 
 
   document_group_info: null 
 
   has_fields: false 
 
   original_owner: true 
 
    So the document does not appear to have been sent, invited, signed, or added to a document group, and it appears to be owned by the API user.    Can you help confirm why this document is opening in View Only mode? I asked for API support within the chatbot, but have not received an answer as of yet.               Nicole Garofalo     Software Engineer          ngarofalo@torticity.com

I worked as kitchen porter with Brambles LTD at Cliffs of Moher    I worked for the company from 23 April 2026 to 7 May 2026.     During this period, I worked a total of 71.75 hours at a rate of €14.15 per hour.     Before starting work, I was not informed of the full terms and conditions of the contract. After reviewing the contract provided to me, I do not agree with its terms and therefore I do not wish to sign it.     I am confirming that I am ending my work without signing this contract.  On top of all that, I was forced to do work that wasn't part of my job description    However, I expect full payment for all hours already worked. The total payment should be calculated based on 71.75 hours at the agreed rate of €14.15 per hour.     Please confirm the payment date for my worked hours.     If payment for the worked period is not made, I will have to seek assistance through the Workplace Relations Commission and other relevant authorities regarding unpaid wages and employment rights.     I hope this matter can be resolved professionally and without further action.  Kind regards Khakimov Mykhailo

Hello                 I trust this email finds you in good spirits.  We have contributing guest posts to our platform,  https://theusaleaders.com/ .  We are thrilled to inform you that we are keen to move forward with your submission. To proceed, we kindly request a draft or post from your end. This will allow us to review it internally with our editorial team for approval. Upon approval, we will promptly publish it on our website and share the live link with you.  For your reference, please find the pricing details below:   General Post:  $300 USD per post (Maximum of 2 do-follow links allowed per post)  Casinos/Crypto/Gambling/  CBD: $600 USD per post    Understanding that your objectives may extend beyond   Theusaleader.com   we are pleased to offer you access to other high-traffic websites where we can publish your articles at the same rates:       No.      Website Name      Website URL      DA       1    Mirror Review     https://www.mirrorreview.com     64      2    Business APAC     https://www.businessapac.com/     58      3    The Education Magazine     https://www.theeducationmagazine.com/     61      4    The USA Leaders     https://theusaleaders.com/     52              Important point to notice   :    1.   Immediate payment after publishing the article.  (we will wait for 3 days and after that your link will be removed.)    2.   Link will be active for 1 year   (we don’t do permanent posting on our website.    As we look to maintain consistency and quality across all guest articles published on our platform, I’ve compiled a set of clear guidelines for contributors. These guidelines will help you communicate our expectations effectively when discussing guest article opportunities with potential writers or partners.            Each post can include a maximum of  two do-follow links . Clearly specify which links are do-follow.    The same do-follow link cannot be repeated within a single post. We require different links.    Include one featured image, if necessary. A maximum of two additional sub-images can be added.    Links will remain active for one year.  We do not offer permanent link placements  on our website.    Immediate payment is required after the article is published. If payment is not made within three days, your link will be removed.    If the client delays payment, an additional charge will be applied for the same article.    Submissions should be between  [800-1500 words] .    Articles must be original, well-written, and free from plagiarism and AI detection free.    For writing, maintain a professional tone and use clear language.    Structure your article for readability by using headings, subheadings, bullet points, and short paragraphs.    All submissions will be reviewed by our editorial team. If your article is approved, we will notify you via email.     Once again, thank you for choosing our website as your preferred platform. We eagerly anticipate the submission of your draft/post and eagerly await the opportunity to embark on a successful partnership.  Best regards,

tickets.body:
Hi SignNow Support, 
 We're experiencing a bug with our Team Documents folder in our Big Little Gyms workspace. 
 The folder header shows 4 documents exist, but when we navigate into the folder the view is completely empty with the message: "There are currently no shared documents in this folder." 
 Workspace details: - Workspace name: Big Little Gyms - Team: BLG Sales Team - 6 members 
 Additional context: - Our Workspace Settings have "Automatically add document copies sent from Team templates to the Team space" toggled ON - The default save location is set to Team Documents - Despite this, the folder appears empty visually even though the document count shows 4 
 This is causing our AR/billing team to be unable to access signed client documents without the sender manually sharing each one individually — which defeats the purpose of the shared team folder. 
 Can you please investigate why the Team Documents folder is showing a count of 4 but rendering no documents, and confirm whether this is a display bug or a permissions/sync issue? 
 Thank you

tickets.body:
Hi, I’m working with SignNow integrated with Salesforce. 
 Currently, when documents are completed, we are receiving **individual signed PDFs via email**. 
 Our requirement is: 
 * All completed documents should be **returned as a single combined package (one PDF)** instead of separate files. * Ideally, this package should be **sent back or synced to Salesforce as one record/file**. 
 We tried using Workspaces, but emails are still coming as separate PDFs. 
 Could you clarify: 
 1. Is there a native setting or configuration to **combine completed documents into one package**? 2. Does this require using **Document Groups, Templates, or Merge settings**? 3. If not available by default, is there any **recommended approach or workaround** (e.g., API, integration setting)? 
 I’m only looking to understand the available options and best approach. 
 Thanks! ​
tickets.ai_title: combine signed documents in one pdf

Hey Lynne, support team, hope you're doing well!  
 
   
 We're a SignNow enterprise customer and looking to understand if there's a way to implement something a little bit dynamic with the signature fields. 
 
   
 I have a template in SignNow that looks like this: 
 
   
 
  
 
   
  
 It's got a mix of signature/date fields for the user to  sign, then some  smart text fields  that  I populate with  the  /integration/object/smartfields endpoint, and even some pre-filled data  that I'm populating  with  the / prefill -texts endpoint.     
  
   
 Specifically, the issue I'm running into is with populating this section below, which needs a signature ONLY in specific cases - if the user lives in Wisconsin. 
 
   
 
  
 
   
 
 Question:  how can I dynamically show a signature field? I know that there is an "Add Conditional Rule" on signature fields, but I want the Conditional Rule to depend on data like the Pre-Filled Text Fields from the API, not data that the user enters in during the signature process. 
 
   
 d  
 
 
   
 Please let me know if that makes sense. Thank you so much for any help.

Hello,            I’m reaching out regarding an unexpected charge on my account. I was billed my annual subscription fee of $180 on April 13th, and I also received an additional charge of $180 today, bringing the total to $360.  The notification I received indicated that I had sent over 300 invites this month. However, I have not sent individual invites. Instead, I have been using a shared invite link to collect signatures for our church’s summer youth camp forms.  Since my subscription renewal, I have collected 117 completed and signed documents. While the link has been accessed multiple times, many users did not complete or sign the forms. Could you please clarify whether each click on the invite link is being counted as an “invite,” even if no form is submitted?  If so, I would appreciate guidance on how to configure my account to prevent these additional charges. Our organization operates on a limited budget, and we can only sustain the $180 annual subscription. We did not encounter this issue last year while using the same process.  At this time, I would also like to request a refund for the additional $180 charge. If maintaining the account within our budget is not possible, we may need to consider canceling your services completely.  Thank you for your assistance. I look forward to your clarification and support in resolving this issue.  Sincerely, Katie Checketts
tickets.subject: Question About Recent Billing Charge

tickets.body: I'm trying to sign a document

Hello,            We are experiencing recurring issues with documents becoming locked in SignNow due to the way signing workflows work in institutional environments.    In many cases, we do not have the email address of the final authorized signer at the time the document is created. Instead, documents are handled by administrative staff (e.g. secretaries or assistants), who coordinate the signing process on behalf of their supervisors. The person who receives and manages the document is therefore not always the person who must legally sign it.    For example:      We receive a document from an administrative contact acting on behalf of the final signer. 
  The document is signed internally using SignNow. 
  After the internal signature is completed, the document must be forwarded to the final signer. 
  At this stage, the document becomes locked and cannot accept additional signatures, because the final signer was not defined in advance. 
    This workflow reflects real institutional processes, but SignNow currently assumes that:      the identity and email address of every signer are known upfront, and 
  the person who receives the email is the same person who signs the document. 
    As a result, we are unable to:      route the document to the final signer after an initial signature, or 
  allow administrative staff to manage the document without signing on behalf of someone else. 
    Adding the intermediary as a signer is not an option, as they are not authorized to sign for the final signer.     Impact 
    This limitation forces us to:      stop the signing process before completion, 
  export documents as PDFs, and 
  complete signatures outside of SignNow, 
    which defeats the purpose of using an electronic signing platform for institutional workflows.     Questions / Request for Guidance 
    We would like your guidance on the following:      Is there a supported way to handle  delegated or secretary-managed signing , where the final signer is known by name but not by email at the time of document creation? 
  Is there a way to prevent documents from becoming permanently locked when additional authorized signatures are still required? 
  Are there recommended best practices or features (e.g. shared inboxes, delegated signing, or flexible signer assignment) for government or institutional use cases? 
    Thank you for your Support.    Best regards,    Iulian    __________________________________________________________                             Iulian Stanciu        Analyste Informatique / IT Analyst        Commission Scolaire Western Quebec        Western Quebec School Board        15 Katimavik, Gatineau (QC) J9J0E9         819 684-2336 | 1 800 363-0111              www.westernquebec.ca
tickets.subject: Issue with locked documents when final signer is unknown and documents are handled by intermediaries

tickets.body:
Good morning. How are you? I hope you are well. We are preaching to people online. Give thanks to Jehovah, for he is good; his loyal love endures forever. Psalm 107:1😇🧑  
   
 
     भविष्य के बारे में आपकी क्या राय है?  
  
     
 
  क्या दुनिया के हालात . . .  
 
   ऐसे ही रहेंगे?   
   और खराब होंगे?   
   बेहतर होंगे?   
 
 
 
 
 
   इस बारे में पवित्र शास्त्र में क्या लिखा है? 
  ‘परमेश्वर इंसानों की आँखों से हर आँसू पोंछ देगा और न मौत रहेगी, न मातम, न रोना-बिलखना, न ही दर्द रहेगा। पिछली बातें खत्म हो चुकी हैं।’— प्रकाशितवाक्य 21:3, 4 .  
 
 
 अगर ऐसी बात है, तो हमारा आनेवाला कल कैसा होगा? 
 
 हमें अपने हर काम में खुशी मिलेगी।— यशायाह 65:21-23 . 
 बीमारी या दुख-तकलीफें नहीं रहेंगी।— यशायाह 25:8;   33:24 . 
 हम हमेशा के लिए अपने परिवार और दोस्तों के साथ रह पाएँगे।— भजन 37:11,   29 . 
     
 
 
 
 
   हम इस बात पर क्यों यकीन कर सकते हैं? 
 
 ज़रा इन दो कारणों पर ध्यान दीजिए: 
 
    परमेश्वर के पास इस वादे को पूरा करने की ताकत है।   पवित्र शास्त्र में यहोवा परमेश्वर को ही “सर्वशक्तिमान” कहा गया है, क्योंकि उसके पास बहुत ताकत है। ( प्रकाशितवाक्य 15:3 ) इसलिए वह अपने वादे के मुताबिक दुनिया के हालात बदल सकता है। “परमेश्वर के लिए सबकुछ मुमकिन है।”— मत्ती 19:26 .  
    परमेश्वर अपने इस वादे को पूरा करने की इच्छा रखता है।   शास्त्र में लिखा है कि यहोवा मौत की नींद सो रहे लोगों को ज़िंदा करने के लिए तरस रहा है। ( अय्यूब 14:14, 15 ) उसमें यह भी बताया गया है कि जो लोग बहुत दुखी हैं, वह उनके करीब रहता है और उनकी मदद करता है। ( भजन 34:18 ) इससे हम यकीन कर सकते हैं कि वह चाहता है कि हमारा आनेवाला कल खुशियों से भरा हो।— भजन 72:12-14;   145:16;   2 पतरस 3:9 .  
 
 
 
 
 
  ज़रा सोचिए  
 
     
 परमेश्वर दुनिया के हालात कैसे बेहतर करेगा? 
 इस सवाल का जवाब पवित्र शास्त्र की इन आयतों में दिया गया है:  दानिय्येल 2:44  और  नीतिवचन 2:21, 22 . 
   
      हमारी सभाओं के बारे में जानिए। पता कीजिए कि आपके इलाके में किस जगह सभाएँ होती हैं।      
   सबसे पास की जगह ढूँढ़ें    (opens new window)   
   
 
   JW.ORG

tickets.body: Hello,     I would appreciate it if you would answer the following questions:   1. I need to use eSignature at most two months per year (not necessarily consecutive months).   Does your Business plan allow me to pay for two month (currently $8.00 per month), or do I have to pay $96.00 per year, knowing that I will only use the plan for at most two months?  2. If your system allows me to pay two out of 12 months, how would I do that (e.g. choose Business Plan at the beginning of the month and cancel it at the end of the month)?  3. Security of data: The documents that I upload for eSigning contain sensitive data that primarily belong to the signers, and should only be viewable by myself and the other signers.  a. When I upload a document, does your system encrypt the uploaded document? b. When the signers, including me, try to sign the document, is the document unencrypted during the signing session, and re-encrypted after each signer finishes their own signing session? c. Can your employees view unencrypted documents?  4. User signing session prematurely terminated: Assuming a document requires a user to sign on more than one line:  (a). If they disagree with the contents of the document, can they back out of the signing session even before signing the first instance of the signature line?  Does the Audit Trail show that fact in sufficient detail?  (b). In a signing session, if the user signs in one or more spots in the document before they commit their signing session, can they revoke the signatures and back out of the signing sesion?  Does the Audit Trail reflect that fact differently than (a) above?  (c). If they finally sign the document in full in another signing session, after one or more signing sessions that they had backed out of, does the Audit Trail still reflect the back-out events and final signing session?     Regards,  Majid Maleki

Witness signing process – i understand SignNow does not have witness signing process. We’ve improvised and have asked signors whose signature must be witnessed to also let us know the name/email of the witnesses, so that the signor and witness gets the signing request from us. Ideally the witness signing process should be: Primary signatory gets signing request as is normal BUT with a requirement to insert name/email of the witness to complete signing. Witness gets the request to sign as witness after primary signatory. 
 I was informed by your team that a workaround here is to send them over as "signing links". When there is more than one signor, the person who opens the link can choose the roles and send invites to the recipients. (i.e., setting it up for himself and the witness). Can you explain this process better?
